title: Analytics - Firmware Update Started
scenario: Firebase analytics event "firmware_update_started" is logged with default and device-specific fields when DFU process starts on a Poly device
steps: 1. Open the Firebase console and navigate to the Events page for the Poly Studio Mobile app.

2. Locate the "firmware_update_started" event and verify the page loads with the following default elements: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment.
3. On the Android device, open the Poly Studio Mobile app and go to the Home screen.
4. Connect to a supported Poly Bluetooth audio device (e.g., Poly Voyager Focus 2).
5. Initiate a DFU (firmware update) process via the app (Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update → Check for Update / Start Update).
6. Wait for the DFU process to begin.
7. Return to the Firebase console and refresh the "firmware_update_started" event page.
8. Verify that the event count has increased (may take up to a few hours to reflect).
9. Inspect the event details and confirm the following device-specific fields are present: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, and Target Language ID.
expected: The Firebase "firmware_update_started" event page loads with all default elements (graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment). After starting a DFU on a supported Poly device, the event count increases and the event includes device-specific fields: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, and Target Language ID.
